hero.subtitle: Agent Engineering Daemon — manage coding agents from Telegram or Discord. 6 backends, fleet mode, agent collaboration, zero babysitting.

pairs.problem: Close the terminal, agent goes offline
pairs.solution: System daemon — survives reboots, zero babysitting

pairs.problem: One terminal = one project, no multitasking
pairs.solution: One bot on Telegram & Discord, unlimited projects in parallel

pairs.problem: Agents work in silos, can't coordinate

features.title: Everything you need to run a fleet

items.name: Fleet Mode
items.desc: N projects, each a Telegram Forum Topic. Independent sessions with crash recovery.

items.name: Multi-Backend
items.desc: Claude Code, Codex, Antigravity, Kiro CLI, Grok, OpenCode. Switch or mix freely.

items.name: Agent Collaboration
items.desc: MCP tools for discovery, delegation, and result reporting between agents.

items.name: Mobile Control
items.desc: Approve tool use, restart sessions, manage your fleet from Telegram inline buttons.

items.name: Cost Guard
items.desc: Per-instance daily spending limits. Auto-pause and alerts before bill shock.

items.name: Persistent Schedules
items.desc: Cron-based tasks backed by SQLite. Survives restarts.

items.name: Web View
items.desc: Real-time terminal streaming in the browser. Drag-sort, profiles, tag groups.

items.name: Settings UI
items.desc: Web-based fleet config. Agent-centric design, validate-before-write.

items.name: Multi-Bot
items.desc: Multiple persona bots in the same Discord channel with owner-wins routing.

items.name: i18n
items.desc: Full EN/繁中 localization. Auto-detect from timezone or fleet.yaml.

items.name: Config Validator
items.desc: agend validate — catch config errors before they crash your fleet.

items.name: Model Failover
items.desc: Auto-switch to backup model on rate limits with 5-minute cooldown.

items.name: Voice Messages
items.desc: Groq Whisper transcription — talk to your agents.

items.name: HTML Export
items.desc: Export sessions as standalone HTML for sharing or archiving.

quickstart.title: Up and running in minutes

oneLiner.label: One-liner (macOS / Linux)
oneLiner.note: Installs Node.js (via nvm), tmux, agend, then launches the wizard. Or step through manually below.

quickstart.footer: Open Telegram, send a message, start coding from your phone.

howitworks.title: From your phone to your fleet

steps.label: You
steps.desc: Send a message to your Telegram or Discord bot

steps.label: General Topic
steps.desc: Interprets natural language, routes to the right agent

steps.label: Agent Fleet
steps.desc: Isolated tmux sessions, each with its own project and backend

steps.label: Collaborate
steps.desc: Agents delegate tasks, share context via MCP tools

steps.label: Results
steps.desc: Flow back to your chat. Permissions arrive as inline buttons.

backends.title: Supported backends
backends.subtitle: Any CLI that reads stdin/stdout. Extensible via the CliBackend interface.

items.a: Yes. Open source, MIT license. You only pay for the AI API usage.
Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Antigravity CLI, Kiro CLI, Grok Build, and OpenCode. Add your own via the CliBackend interface.
Telegram and Discord. Both support topic/channel-based fleet routing, plus ClassicBot mode — run /start in any channel to attach an agent — and Telegram forum topic mode for one topic per project.
Yes. Full support including forum topics, slash commands, ClassicBot (/start in any channel), and multi-bot same-channel (several persona bots in one channel with owner-wins routing).
Yes, via WSL. A one-click PowerShell installer sets up WSL 2, your AI backends, AgEnD, and auto-start — see the Windows Installation Guide.
No. Runs on any macOS, Linux, or Windows (WSL) machine with tmux. Install as a system service for auto-start on boot.
Via MCP tools: send_to_instance, delegate_task, report_result. Agents discover each other, delegate work, and report results — all peer-to-peer.
A natural language dispatcher. Send any task to the General topic — it figures out which agent should handle it and delegates automatically.

items.q: How much does it cost to run?
items.a: AgEnD itself is free. Costs come from AI API usage. Use cost guards to set daily per-instance spending limits.

items.q: Can I connect my local Claude Code?
items.a: Yes. External sessions connect via IPC socket and appear as fleet members. See the docs for setup.

footer.tagline: The operations layer for AI coding agents.
